notes: I was able to launch the game and compile all the shaders. I went to congifure my settings and turned on raytracing. This crashes the game and I was not able to launch the game again. After some looking, I deleted all the user files for the game thinking that would reset those setting but that didn't work. The only thing that worked was deleting the compatadata file. This reset all the settings to default. I was then able to start the game. It ran fine through the opening scenese, but as soon as the game had actually starts it crashes. I checked the crash report and it is some TDR error. I tried several different things to get this running and it just wont. It sounds like the only thing that may work is getting a patch file that bypasses EAC, but that's a little much for me, plus that would only work for offline use. I've tried it with proton experimental, proton GE, and CachyOS Proton. This game is borked for me at least. Sounds like some others have had better luck.
title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2

notes: After fixes, it runs great.  The main thing is PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1  __GL_THREADED_OPTIMIZATIONS=1 seems to be the big fix.

title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2

notes:
Game worked as of May 3, 2026 while using Proton Experimental, with a bit worse performance than Windows. This was the last time I tried playing the game until today (May 21, 2026).

However, as of today and the launch of the 13.0 patch, the game doesn't get past the initial menu start-up screen as it keeps on crashing when trying to get past the start-up screen. 

I tried using Proton 9.0-4, Proton Experimential, proton-cachyos-11.0-20260506, Proton Hotfix and not using any compatibility layer at all and none of those worked.
title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti Laptop GPU
notes:
Issue Description
The game currently suffers from an unavoidable crash to desktop (CTD) during the initial story_intro scene, right before transitioning to the main menu (title_scene). This issue stems from a critical rendering deadlock introduced in recent game patches, making it impossible to reach the gameplay loop on certain hardware configurations under Proton.

Technical Breakdown & Log Analysis
An extraction and analysis of the game's crash dump (error.yaml) reveals the exact failure point:

YAML
"message": "GPU crash: reason: TDR, marker: COMPUTE_INST_INDEX_BEGIN"
"file": "E:\\_\\_task_dir\\common\\code\\drv\\source\\video\\vid_crash_report.cpp"
"line": 78
"active scene": "story_intro"
"memory info": "Title memory: 3079.8/10629.2 mb (4214.0 free)"
1. The TDR Trigger (COMPUTE_INST_INDEX_BEGIN)
The crash is explicitly flagged as a TDR (Timeout Detection and Recovery) event. The GPU engine hangs at the specific pipeline marker COMPUTE_INST_INDEX_BEGIN. This indicates that the Saber Interactive engine (Magnus) dispatches a low-level Compute Shader command related to geometry indexing or streaming during the intro cinematic. Under Proton, this specific compute pass triggers an infinite loop or unsupported instruction execution in the Vulkan driver layer, causing the kernel to forcibly reset the GPU context after a timeout.

2. Memory Allocation Misconception
Initially, the crash appears similar to an Out-of-Memory (OOM) error because the engine logs 0.0 free inside specific internal texture streaming pools. However, forcing lowest graphical settings or attempting to artificially pad available VRAM via dxvk.conf variables (dxgi.maxDeviceMemory, vkd3d.maxDeviceMemory) does not resolve the issue. Even when the engine acknowledges ample headroom---such as 4214.0 mb free---the TDR occurs at the exact same compute instruction marker.

3. Low-Level Hardware Polling Avoids Workarounds
Attempts to bypass or modify the hardware profile via standard DXGI or NVAPI environment overrides (such as PROTON_DISABLE_NVAPI=1 or WINEDLLOVERRIDES="nvapi,nvapi64=d") are completely bypassed by the game engine. In recent updates, the engine implements direct queries via vkGetPhysicalDeviceProperties to the native Vulkan driver (libvulkan_nvidia.so). It fetches the raw hardware limits and device name directly from the host system, overriding any high-level virtualization configs set in the Proton prefix.

4. Hardcoded Media Assets
Because the game packages all cinematic assets directly inside compressed, proprietary archive structures (.pak files under client_pc/root/paks/), it is impossible to selectively delete or rename the individual .bk2 (Bink Video) files to force-skip the problematic intro scene.

Conclusion & Current Verdict
Unplayable / Broken by recent patch.

The current version of the game forces a low-level compute pass during the mandatory intro sequence that causes an unrecoverable GPU hang under Proton for mobile NVIDIA GPUs, independent of the actual VRAM availability or graphics configuration. Until either a game patch modifies this specific compute execution path or a subsequent VKD3D-Proton / NVIDIA driver update implements a specific translation fix for this instruction, a refund is recommended for users experiencing this loop.
title: Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2
